ADR-001.title: Adopt upstream-shaped CUE KG entries through the kg CLI
ADR-001.context: The repository already has a Codex drift KG, but needs the upstream full-example project knowledge shape.
ADR-001.decision: Use the local kg CLI to create and validate .kb entries for decisions, patterns, insights, rejected approaches, and a derived _index.
ADR-001.rationale: The CLI owns the upstream module wiring and keeps the local project KG aligned with quicue.ca/kg conventions.
consequences: Project knowledge is exported with kg index --full.
The Codex drift KG remains under .kg/codex with a separate authority boundary.

ADR-002.title: Keep agent KG access read-only by default
ADR-002.context: Agents need fast access to KG facts but should not treat derived indexes, MCP output, or generated observations as mutation authority.
ADR-002.decision: Provide a repo-local skill that routes agents through kg vet, kg index, kg query, kg settle, and read-only MCP inspection before editing KG authority.
ADR-002.rationale: The CLI and existing MCP policy already model read-only KG access; the skill makes that workflow explicit for future agents.
consequences: Agents should run kg vet and kg index before changing .kb entries.
Mutation still happens through ordinary file edits reviewed by repository validation.

ADR-003.title: Canonicalize project KG directory boundaries
ADR-003.context: The repository has a reusable kg schema package, a repo-local project knowledge graph, and a separate Codex drift-control graph.
ADR-003.decision: Use .kb as the only project knowledge instance directory; reserve .kg/codex for Codex drift controls and treat kg/ as reusable schema and examples.
ADR-003.rationale: A single instance directory avoids adapter and documentation drift while preserving the framework-versus-instance split.
consequences: Project knowledge entries, indexes, and kg CLI validation remain under .kb.
Codex drift-control surfaces remain under .kg/codex and are not a .kg alias for project knowledge.
Executable adapters must name which graph authority they expose.

INSIGHT-001.statement: CUE unification can be both the schema validator and the KG federation mechanism.
evidence: The upstream full example indexes decisions, insights, rejected approaches, and patterns through one CUE value.
The local kg CLI validates .kb entries and exports a derived _index without a database.
INSIGHT-001.implication: The repo-local KG can stay zero-infrastructure and still be queryable by agents.
action_items: Keep kg index --full exportable through the validator.

INSIGHT-002.statement: The Codex drift KG and upstream-shaped project KG serve different roles.
evidence: .kg/codex declares watchdog and MCP control surfaces.
.kb declares project knowledge entries and derived indexes through the kg CLI.
INSIGHT-002.implication: Agents should query both surfaces, but should not merge their authority boundaries.

REJ-001.approach: Import quicue.ca/kg directly into the root lattice CUE module for the example.
REJ-001.reason: That would couple root validation to a remote module dependency for a small project knowledge example.
REJ-001.alternative: Use kg init so .kb is its own CUE module with the CLI-managed quicue.ca/kg schema link.

REJ-002.reason: .kg/codex owns Codex drift controls, watchdogs, and MCP declarations; mixing project KG entries there would blur authority boundaries.
REJ-002.alternative: Use .kb for project knowledge and keep .kg/codex for drift controls.

REJ-003.approach: Treat .kg as a deprecated alias for the project knowledge graph.
REJ-003.reason: This repository already uses .kg/codex for Codex drift-control authority, so a broad .kg alias would blur the project knowledge and control-plane boundaries.
REJ-003.alternative: Use .kb for project knowledge and keep .kg/codex as the only .kg subtree with KG authority.

struct-as-set.name: Struct-as-Set
struct-as-set.problem: List-valued membership fields admit duplicates and make drift checks harder to unify.
struct-as-set.solution: Represent membership and relationships as {[string]: true} sets.
struct-as-set.context: Use for related entries, used-in fields, and required-path membership.
struct-as-set.example: KG decisions, patterns, insights, and rejected approaches use related structs.

adr-as-cue.name: ADR as CUE Struct
adr-as-cue.problem: Markdown-only decisions are not type-checked or queryable by repo validators.
adr-as-cue.solution: Encode decisions as CUE structs with required context, decision, rationale, and consequences.
adr-as-cue.context: Use for repository architecture choices that should participate in KG indexes.

comprehension-index.name: Comprehension-Derived Index
comprehension-index.problem: Manually maintained summary views drift from source entries.
comprehension-index.solution: Compute summary, status, and confidence views with CUE comprehensions.
comprehension-index.context: Use for KG aggregate views and drift-control inventories.

accepted.ADR-001: Adopt upstream-shaped CUE KG entries through the kg CLI
accepted.ADR-002: Keep agent KG access read-only by default

high.INSIGHT-001: CUE unification can be both the schema validator and the KG federation mechanism.

medium.INSIGHT-002: The Codex drift KG and upstream-shaped project KG serve different roles.
